ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Takiyah A. Smith (Trustee). Malik Smith was also present.

Ms. Navia noted that Malik Smith was present today in court and that it takes a few days advanced notice to the Trustee to make arrangements for Malik. Ms. Navia addressed the second supplemental petition, stated that both Ms. Smith and Malik reside at the Geneva address, that the bond should be reduced to $25,000.00, and that the Trustee's fees were reduced to $1,509.75 per the Examiner's notes. At the conclusion of Ms. Navia's comments, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-10-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Takiyah Smith.

Ms. Navia stated that she believes that In House Support Services will be consistent going forward and that they will be looking into Golden Gate Regional Services and other services that may be available for Malik. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Continued To Aug-28-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-19-2012 Continued To Jul-31-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with Takiyah Smith.

The Court informed the Parties that the assessment fees should be/need to be paid. Greer Smith stated that the $20,000.00 request is a one time issue. Takiyah Smith stated that the amount is for three and a half years of care, that the Conservatorship fees are minimal and that in the future she will not be asking for as much for care taking services. The Court requested a further declaration with regard to services provided other than care taking services, other special needs services and envisioning a plan/budget for the future as well as how past hours fall into special needs. The accounting petition was continued to July 31, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Andrade.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for Takiyah Smith (Trustee).

Ms. Greer stated that she should be able to have the accounting filed within 30-days. Status of accounting was continued to February 15, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
